competitors.label: MDN Hoisting
competitors.selectionReason: MDN's hoisting glossary page is the canonical docs reference for how different declarations behave before execution.
theirStrengths: It distinguishes declaration types precisely and links hoisting to the broader language model.
theirGaps: It is less explicitly optimized around debugging and interview misconceptions than our trivia voice.

realWorldUseCases: A feature module may call a helper before its function expression is assigned, or instantiate a class before its declaration line. Those failures look similar at first, but they come from different hoisting outcomes.
actionableExamples: speak(); // TypeError: speak is not a function

realWorldUseCases: MDN explains declaration behavior well, but our page now anchors the topic more clearly in frontend debugging symptoms.
actionableExamples: Its examples are precise, while our page now puts function declaration, var function expression, and class behavior side by side.
followUpConfusion: MDN still has broader docs depth, so the overall follow-up comparison remains close.
Add one direct cross-link or note that separates classic hoisting confusion from TDZ errors so the follow-up set is broader.

competitors.winReason: MDN remains the canonical definition, but our page is now stronger on the concrete debugging symptoms and the side-by-side function-expression/class failures developers actually hit.

competitors.label: GreatFrontEnd Hoisting pitfalls
competitors.selectionReason: GreatFrontEnd has a directly relevant interview-prep question focused on hoisting pitfalls.
theirStrengths: It is closely aligned with the interview angle and already focuses on pitfalls rather than definition only.
theirGaps: It appears narrower than docs pages that unpack multiple declaration categories in detail.

realWorldUseCases: A feature module may call a helper before its function expression is assigned, or instantiate a class before its declaration line. Those failures look similar at first, but they come from different hoisting outcomes.
followUpConfusion: Function declarations are callable early because the function value is ready during setup.

realWorldUseCases: GreatFrontEnd focuses on pitfalls, but our page now pushes further into concrete production-style failure modes.
actionableExamples: Its pitfall examples remain competitive enough that the example criterion stays close.
followUpConfusion: Our page now separates function declarations, var function expressions, and class TDZ behavior more explicitly.

actionableExamples: Add one tiny matrix that compares function declarations, function expressions, and classes in one glance.

competitors.winReason: GreatFrontEnd is a strong interview comparator, but our page now does more to map hoisting to real debugging outcomes and declaration-type-specific failures.

competitors.label: GeeksforGeeks Hoisting in JavaScript
competitors.selectionReason: This is a common tutorial-style comparator that covers the standard hoisting explanation and examples.
competitors.winReason: Our page is sharper on under-the-hood debugging signals and common misreadings of hoisting, while GeeksforGeeks stays more introductory.
theirStrengths: It gives the standard introductory explanation and beginner-friendly examples.
theirGaps: It is lighter on the debugging lens and on the distinction between different failing declaration types.

realWorldUseCases: That under-the-hood setup explains several debug traps: var reads as undefined, function declarations work early, and function expressions or classes fail for different reasons.
followUpConfusion: Hoisting is not code movement. It is how the engine prepares scope before running the script, registering declarations first.

realWorldUseCases: GeeksforGeeks explains hoisting correctly, but it spends less time on the concrete debugging traps that show up in production code.
actionableExamples: Its short examples are serviceable enough that the example criterion remains close.
followUpConfusion: It is less explicit about why different declaration forms fail in different ways before initialization.

actionableExamples: Add one combined matrix or trace that compares var, function declaration, function expression, and class reads before declaration.
